title: The Sense of Wonder
excerpt: A gentle, profound meditation on how to nurture a child's innate sense of wonder through shared exploration of the natural world.
keyIdeas: Children are born with a natural sense of wonder that adults can either nurture or extinguish
Parents need not be naturalists; they need only share their child's curiosity
Nature is the richest classroom for developing observation, imagination, and empathy
The adult who preserves a sense of wonder stays connected to the deepest sources of joy
keyTakeaways: You do not need to know the names of things to appreciate their beauty
Walking on a beach at night, watching a storm, following a woodland stream — these are acts of discovery
The sense of wonder is the foundation of both science and art
Protecting nature matters because it protects the capacity for wonder in future generations
whoShouldRead: Parents and educators of young children
Anyone who has lost touch with the joy of outdoor exploration
Teachers of early childhood and environmental education
Fans of Carson's other works seeking her most personal writing
whoShouldSkip: Those seeking a scientific or ecological treatise
Readers without interest in children or parenting
